slides.title: Your heart has a *quiet ally*: laughter.
slides.sub: A genuine laugh sets off a chemical that relaxes your blood vessels. Here is the science.

slides.title: Laughter signals the lining
slides.body: A real, unforced laugh tells the inner lining of your blood vessels, the endothelium, to *ease and open up*.

slides.title: Nitric oxide is the key
slides.body: That signal triggers nitric oxide, a molecule that *relaxes vessel walls* so blood can flow more freely.

slides.title: Better endothelial function
slides.body: Studies link genuine laughter to improved endothelial function, the same response *can support* healthier circulation.

slides.title: Stress does the opposite
slides.body: Chronic stress tends to stiffen vessels. A warm laugh *may help* offset that, gently and for free.

slides.title: Make room for joy
slides.body: Time with people who make you laugh is real heart care. It is *one of the simplest habits* you can keep.

slides.title: Small joys, *tracked daily*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your sleep, food, movement and steps into one daily Health Score, so the habits that protect your heart stay visible.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A real belly laugh is doing more for your heart than you think. 😄🌿

Genuine laughter triggers nitric oxide, which relaxes your blood vessels and can support healthier circulation. Stress does the opposite. Save this for the next slow day. 🔖

youtube_title: How Laughter Helps Your Blood Vessels and Heart
